Registry / Terms / sensitivity

sensitivity

—Not in VIM/VIML G 18 #00647 · 13 instances

Suggested actions

  1. HIGH No authoritative definition in VIM or VIML.
  2. MEDIUM 11 distinct definitions — harmonise.

Publication instances

Ed.YearPublicationClauseDefinitionConsistency
20102006OIML R 51-1:2006
#00647
T.3.5
For a given value of the measured mass, the quotient of the change of the observed variable, stem:[l], and the corresponding change of the measured mass, stem:[M]: stem:[k] = stem:[Delta l/Delta M]
pending
20102006OIML R 76-1:2006
#00957
T.4.1
for a given value of the measured mass, the quotient of the change, stem:[Delta]l, of the observed variable, l, and the corresponding change, stem:[Delta]m, of the measured mass, m
pending
20102006OIML R 82:2006
#01057
3.13
Output signal per unit mass of the sample component of interest Peak area is independent of broadening effects in the carrier gas. It is expressed in either one of the two following ways: 3.13.1 With a concentration-dependent detector, the instrument sensitivity, S, (…) 3.13.2 With a mass flow rate dependent detector, the instrument sensitivity, S, (…)
pending
20102000OIML R 60:2000
#00740
2.4.13
ratio of a change in response (output) of a load cell to a corresponding change in the stimulus (load applied)
pending
20101994OIML R 113:1994
#01499
3.12
the output signal of a detector per unit mass of eluted sample components of interest and is either one of the following categories: 3.12.1 Concentration-dependent detector Expressed in A · mL · g–1 or V · mL · g–1, and by the equation: where S = sensitivity P = the integrated peak area (A · s or V · s) F = the carrier gas flow rate (mL · s–1) M = mass of the sample in the carrier gas (g) 3.12.2 Mass-flow-rate-dependent detector Expressed in A · s · g–1 or V · s · g–1, and by the equation: where the symbols are the same as those defined in 3.12.1.
pending
20101982OIML R 53:1982
#00685
T
quotient of the increase in value of displacement of the reference point and the corresponding increase in pressure
pending
202X2021OIML R126-1:2021
#03488
3.1.25
quotient of change in an indication of measuring system and the corresponding change in a value of quantity being measured (OIML V 2-200, 4.12)
pending
202X2006OIML R 51-1:2006
#00647
T.3.5
For a given value of the measured mass, the quotient of the change of the observed variable, l, and the corresponding change of the measured mass, M: k = l / M
pending
202X2006OIML R 76-1:2006
#00957
T.4.1
for a given value of the measured mass, the quotient of the change, Δl, of the observed variable, l, and the corresponding change, Δm, of the measured mass, m
pending
202X2006OIML R 82:2006
#01057
3.13
Output signal per unit mass of the sample component of interest in the carrier gas. It is expressed in either one of the two following ways: 3.13.1 With a concentration-dependent detector, the instrument sensitivity, S, is expressed as: A · mL · g-1, or V · mL · g-1, and by the equation: S = P · F/ M where: P = the peak area, F = the carrier gas flow rate, M = the mass of the sample in the carrier gas. With the units: A = amperes, mL = millilitres, g = grams, V = volts. 3.13.2 With a mass flow rate dependent detector, the instrument sensitivity, S, is expressed in: A · s · g-1, or V · s · g-1, and by the equation: S = P/ M where the symbols have the same definitions as in 3.13.1, with the additional unit of: s = seconds.
pending
202X1997OIML R 123:1997
#03487
3.7
Change in the response of a measuring instrument divided by the corresponding change in the stimulus.
pending
202X1994OIML R 113:1994
#01499
3.12
the output signal of a detector per unit mass of eluted sample components of interest and is either one of the following categories: 3.12.1 Concentration-dependent detector Expressed in A · mL · g–1 or V · mL · g–1, and by the equation: where S = sensitivity P = the integrated peak area (A · s or V · s) F = the carrier gas flow rate (mL · s–1) M = mass of the sample in the carrier gas (g) 3.12.2 Mass-flow-rate-dependent detector Expressed in A · s · g–1 or V · s · g–1, and by the equation: where the symbols are the same as those defined in 3.12.1.
pending
202X1982OIML R 53:1982
#00685
T.13
quotient of the increase in value of displacement of the reference point and the corresponding increase in pressure
pending
